Question? Give us feedback → (opens in a new tab)Edit this page
Gateways
Authentication

Gateway Authentication

Gateways can be protected with authentication to control who can discover and invoke tools. apifai supports multiple authentication methods that can be configured per gateway.

Authentication Methods

MethodDescriptionUse Case
NoneNo authentication requiredPublic/development gateways
API KeyHeader or query parameter keySimple machine-to-machine auth
Bearer TokenJWT bearer tokenUser-scoped access
Basic AuthUsername and passwordLegacy integrations
OAuth2OAuth 2.0 client credentialsEnterprise integrations

Configuring Authentication

Via the UI

  1. Navigate to the gateway detail page
  2. Open the Authentication tab
  3. Click Add Auth Method
  4. Select the method type and configure

Via the API

curl -X POST https://api.apif.ai/gateways/{id}/auth \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "type": "api_key",
    "configuration": {
      "headerName": "X-API-Key"
    }
  }'

API Key Authentication

Generate API keys for programmatic access to your gateway:

Generate a Key

curl -X POST https://api.apif.ai/gateways/{id}/auth/api-keys \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "name": "Production Key",
    "scopes": ["tools:read", "tools:execute"],
    "expiresAt": "2027-01-01T00:00:00Z"
  }'

Response (key is only shown once):

{
  "id": "key-uuid",
  "name": "Production Key",
  "key": "apifai_gw_abc123...",
  "scopes": ["tools:read", "tools:execute"],
  "expiresAt": "2027-01-01T00:00:00Z"
}

Using the Key

# Via header
curl https://api.apif.ai/mcp/acme/my-tools \
  -H "X-API-Key: apifai_gw_abc123..."
 
# Via Authorization header
curl https://api.apif.ai/mcp/acme/my-tools \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer apifai_gw_abc123..."

List Keys

curl https://api.apif.ai/gateways/{id}/auth/api-keys \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN"

Revoke a Key

curl -X DELETE https://api.apif.ai/gateways/{id}/auth/api-keys/{keyId} \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN"

Key Scopes

ScopeDescription
tools:readList and view tool definitions
tools:executeExecute/invoke tools
gateway:readView gateway configuration

Multiple Auth Methods

A gateway can have multiple auth methods enabled simultaneously. Requests are authenticated if they match any of the configured methods.

This allows gradual migration between auth methods:

  1. Add the new auth method
  2. Update clients to use the new method
  3. Remove the old auth method

Security Best Practices

  1. Always use authentication in production gateways
  2. Rotate keys regularly — set expiration dates
  3. Use minimal scopes — grant only the permissions needed
  4. Monitor usage — check Analytics for unusual patterns
  5. Revoke compromised keys immediately — keys cannot be recovered once revoked
SkillsTool Scoping
apifai — Universal API-to-AI Tool Gateway
